verses.latin_vulgate: Initium Evangelii Jesu Christi, Filii Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀρχὴ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut scriptum est in Isaia propheta: [Ecce ego mitto angelum meum ante faciem tuam, qui præparabit viam tuam ante te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ης γέγραπται ἐν τοῖς προφήταις, ἰδού, ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου πρὸ προσώπου σου ὃς κατασκευάσει τὴν ὁδόν σου ἔμπροσθέν σου,
verses.king_james: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vox clamantis in deserto: Parate viam Domini, rectas facite semitas ejus.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φωνὴ βοῶντος ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἑτοιμάσατε τὴν ὁδὸν κυρίου εὐθείας ποιεῖτε τὰς τρίβους αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fuit Joannes in deserto baptizans, et prædicans baptismum pœnitentiæ in remissionem peccatorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο ἰωάννης βαπτίζων ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ καὶ κηρύσσων βάπτισμα μετανοίας εἰς ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν
verses.king_james: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egrediebatur ad eum omnis Judææ regio, et Jerosolymitæ universi, et baptizabantur ab illo in Jordanis flumine, confitentes peccata sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξεπορεύετο πρὸς αὐτὸν πᾶσα ἡ ἰουδαία χώρα καὶ οἱ ἱεροσολυμῖται καὶ ἐβαπτίζοντο πάντες ἐν τῷ ἰορδάνῃ ποταμῷ ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ ἐξομολογούμενοι τὰς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat Joannes vestitus pilis cameli, et zona pellicea circa lumbos ejus, et locustas et mel silvestre edebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἰωάννης ἐνδεδυμένος τρίχας καμήλου καὶ ζώνην δερματίνην περὶ τὴν ὀσφὺν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐσθίων ἀκρίδας καὶ μέλι ἄγριον
verses.king_james: And John was clothed with camel's hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et prædicabat dicens: Venit fortior post me, cujus non sum dignus procumbens solvere corrigiam calceamentorum ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκήρυσσεν λέγων, ἕρχεται ὁ ἰσχυρότερός μου ὀπίσω μου οὗ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἱκανὸς κύψας λῦσαι τὸν ἱμάντα τῶν ὑποδημάτων αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego baptizavi vos aqua, ille vero baptizabit vos Spiritu Sancto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ μὲν ἐβάπτισα ὑμᾶς ἐν ὕδατι αὐτὸς δὲ βαπτίσει ὑμᾶς ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est: in diebus illis venit Jesus a Nazareth Galilææ: et baptizatus est a Joanne in Jordane.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις ἦλθεν ἰησοῦς ἀπὸ ναζαρὲτ τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἐβαπτίσθη ὑπὸ ἰωάννου εἰς τὸν ἰορδάνην
verses.king_james: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim ascendens de aqua, vidit cælos apertos, et Spiritum tamquam columbam descendentem, et manentem in ipso.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθὲως ἀναβαίνων ἀπὸ τοῦ ὕδατος εἶδεν σχιζομένους τοὺς οὐρανοὺς καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα ὡσεὶ περιστερὰν καταβαῖνον ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vox facta est de cælis: Tu es Filius meus dilectus, in te complacui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ φωνὴ ἐγένετο ἐκ τῶν οὐρανῶν σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός ἐν ὦ εὐδόκησα
verses.king_james: And there came a voice from heaven, *saying*, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et statim Spiritus expulit eum in desertum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθὺς τὸ πνεῦμα αὐτὸν ἐκβάλλει εἰς τὴν ἔρημον
verses.king_james: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat in deserto quadraginta diebus, et quadraginta noctibus: et tentabatur a Satana: eratque cum bestiis, et angeli ministrabant illi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ἐκεῖ ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ἡμέρας τεσσαράκοντα πειραζόμενος ὑπὸ τοῦ σατανᾶ καὶ ἦν μετὰ τῶν θηρίων καὶ οἱ ἄγγελοι διηκόνουν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam autem traditus est Joannes, venit Jesus in Galilæam, prædicans Evangelium regni Dei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὸ παραδοθῆναι τὸν ἰωάννην ἦλθεν ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν κηρύσσων τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dicens: Quoniam impletum est tempus, et appropinquavit regnum Dei: pœnitemini, et credite Evangelio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγων ὅτι πεπλήρωται ὁ καιρὸς καὶ ἤγγικεν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ μετανοεῖτε καὶ πιστεύετε ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ
verses.king_james: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et præteriens secus mare Galilææ, vidit Simonem, et Andream fratrem ejus, mittentes retia in mare (erant enim piscatores),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περιπατῶν δὲ παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν τῆς γαλιλαίας εἶδεν σίμωνα καὶ ἀνδρέαν τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὐτοῦ βάλλοντας ἀμφίβληστρον ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ ἦσαν γὰρ ἁλιεῖς
verses.king_james: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit eis Jesus: Venite post me, et faciam vos fieri piscatores hominum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς δεῦτε ὀπίσω μου καὶ ποιήσω ὑμᾶς γενέσθαι ἁλιεῖς ἀνθρώπων
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et protinus relictis retibus, secuti sunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως ἀφέντες τὰ δίκτυα αὐτῶν, ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et progressus inde pusillum, vidit Jacobum Zebedæi, et Joannem fratrem ejus, et ipsos componentes retia in navi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προβὰς ἐκεῖθεν ὀλίγον εἶδεν ἰάκωβον τὸν τοῦ ξεβεδαίου καὶ ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ αὐτοὺς ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ καταρτίζοντας τὰ δίκτυα
verses.king_james: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the *son* of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.

verses.latin_vulgate: et statim vocavit illos. Et relicto patre suo Zebedæo in navi cum mercenariis, secuti sunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθεὼς ἐκάλεσεν αὐτούς καὶ ἀφέντες τὸν πατέρα αὐτῶν ξεβεδαῖον ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ μετὰ τῶν μισθωτῶν ἀπῆλθον ὀπίσω αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingrediuntur Capharnaum: et statim sabbatis ingressus in synagogam, docebat eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσπορεύονται εἰς καπερναούμ καὶ εὐθεὼς τοῖς σάββασιν εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὴν συναγωγὴν ἐδίδασκεν
verses.king_james: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et stupebant super doctrina ejus: erat enim docens eos quasi potestatem habens, et non sicut scribæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξεπλήσσοντο ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ ἦν γὰρ διδάσκων αὐτοὺς ὡς ἐξουσίαν ἔχων καὶ οὐχ ὡς οἱ γραμματεῖς
verses.king_james: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat in synagoga eorum homo in spiritu immundo: et exclamavit,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ αὐτῶν ἄνθρωπος ἐν πνεύματι ἀκαθάρτῳ καὶ ἀνέκραξεν
verses.king_james: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicens: Quid nobis et tibi, Jesu Nazarene? venisti perdere nos? scio qui sis, Sanctus Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγων, ἕα, τί ἡμῖν καὶ σοί, ἰησοῦ ναζαρηνέ ἦλθες ἀπολέσαι ἡμᾶς οἶδά σε τίς εἶ ὁ ἅγιος τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Saying, Let *us* alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et comminatus est ei Jesus, dicens: Obmutesce, et exi de homine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγων, φιμώθητι καὶ ἔξελθε ἐξ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et discerpens eum spiritus immundus, et exclamans voce magna, exiit ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ σπαράξαν αὐτὸν τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἀκάθαρτον καὶ κράξαν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ἐξῆλθεν ἐξ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mirati sunt omnes, ita ut conquirerent inter se dicentes: Quidnam est hoc? quænam doctrina hæc nova? quia in potestate etiam spiritibus immundis imperat, et obediunt ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐθαμβήθησαν παντές, ὥστε συζητεῖν πρὸς αὐτοὺς, λέγοντας τί ἐστιν τοῦτο τις ἡ διδαχὴ ἡ καινὴ αὑτη, ὅτι κατ᾽ ἐξουσίαν καὶ τοῖς πνεύμασιν τοῖς ἀκαθάρτοις ἐπιτάσσει καὶ ὑπακούουσιν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine *is* this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et processit rumor ejus statim in omnem regionem Galilææ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθεν δὲ ἡ ἀκοὴ αὐτοῦ εὐθὺς εἰς ὅλην τὴν περίχωρον τῆς γαλιλαίας
verses.king_james: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et protinus egredientes de synagoga, venerunt in domum Simonis et Andreæ, cum Jacobo et Joanne.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὐθέως ἐκ τῆς συναγωγῆς ἐξελθόντες ἦλθον εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν σίμωνος καὶ ἀνδρέου μετὰ ἰακώβου καὶ ἰωάννου
verses.king_james: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

verses.latin_vulgate: Decumbebat autem socrus Simonis febricitans: et statim dicunt ei de illa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ δὲ πενθερὰ σίμωνος κατέκειτο πυρέσσουσα καὶ εὐθὲως λέγουσιν αὐτῷ περὶ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: But Simon's wife's mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accedens elevavit eam, apprehensa manu ejus: et continuo dimisit eam febris, et ministrabat eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθὼν ἤγειρεν αὐτὴν κρατήσας τῆς χειρὸς αὐτῆς καὶ ἀφῆκεν αὐτὴν ὁ πυρετός εὐθὲως, καὶ διηκόνει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vespere autem facto cum occidisset sol, afferebant ad eum omnes male habentes, et dæmonia habentes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης ὅτε ἔδυ ὁ ἥλιος ἔφερον πρὸς αὐτὸν πάντας τοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας καὶ τοὺς δαιμονιζομένους
verses.king_james: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.

verses.latin_vulgate: et erat omnis civitas congregata ad januam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἡ πόλις ὅλη ἐπισυνηγμένη ἦν πρὸς τὴν θύραν
verses.king_james: And all the city was gathered together at the door.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et curavit multos, qui vexabantur variis languoribus, et dæmonia multa ejiciebat, et non sinebat ea loqui, quoniam sciebant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν πολλοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας ποικίλαις νόσοις καὶ δαιμόνια πολλὰ ἐξέβαλεν καὶ οὐκ ἤφιεν λαλεῖν τὰ δαιμόνια ὅτι ᾔδεισαν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et diluculo valde surgens, egressus abiit in desertum locum, ibique orabat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πρωῒ ἔννυχον λίαν ἀναστὰς ἐξῆλθεν καὶ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς ἔρημον τόπον κἀκεῖ προσηύχετο
verses.king_james: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et prosecutus est eum Simon, et qui cum illo erant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ κατεδίωξάν αὐτὸν ὁ σίμων καὶ οἱ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum invenissent eum, dixerunt ei: Quia omnes quærunt te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὗροντες αὐτὸν λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ὅτι πάντες ζητοῦσίν σε
verses.king_james: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All *men* seek for thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis: Eamus in proximos vicos, et civitates, ut et ibi prædicem: ad hoc enim veni.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἄγωμεν εἰς τὰς ἐχομένας κωμοπόλεις ἵνα κἀκεῖ κηρύξω εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ ἐξελήλυθα
verses.king_james: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erat prædicans in synagogis eorum, et in omni Galilæa, et dæmonia ejiciens.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἦν κηρύσσων ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς αὐτῶν εἰς ὅλην τὴν γαλιλαίαν καὶ τὰ δαιμόνια ἐκβάλλων
verses.king_james: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venit ad eum leprosus deprecans eum: et genu flexo dixit ei: Si vis, potes me mundare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔρχεται πρὸς αὐτὸν λεπρὸς παρακαλῶν αὐτὸν καὶ γονυπετῶν αὐτὸν καὶ λέγων αὐτῷ ὅτι ἐὰν θέλῃς δύνασαί με καθαρίσαι
verses.king_james: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem misertus ejus, extendit manum suam: et tangens eum, ait illi: Volo: mundare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς σπλαγχνισθεὶς ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα ἥψατο αὐτοῦ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ φέλω καθαρίσθητι
verses.king_james: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth *his* hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum dixisset, statim discessit ab eo lepra, et mundatus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰπόντος αὐτοῦ εὐθὲως ἀπῆλθεν ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ, ἡ λέπρα καὶ ἐκαθαρίσθη
verses.king_james: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et comminatus est ei, statimque ejecit illum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμβριμησάμενος αὐτῷ εὐθὲως ἐξέβαλεν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;

verses.latin_vulgate: et dicit ei: Vide nemini dixeris: sed vade, ostende te principi sacerdotum, et offer pro emundatione tua, quæ præcepit Moyses in testimonium illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὅρα μηδενὶ μηδὲν εἴπῃς ἀλλ᾽ ὕπαγε σεαυτὸν δεῖξον τῷ ἱερεῖ καὶ προσένεγκε περὶ τοῦ καθαρισμοῦ σου ἃ προσέταξεν μωσῆς, εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille egressus cœpit prædicare, et diffamare sermonem, ita ut jam non posset manifeste introire in civitatem, sed foris in desertis locis esset, et conveniebant ad eum undique.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐξελθὼν ἤρξατο κηρύσσειν πολλὰ καὶ διαφημίζειν τὸν λόγον ὥστε μηκέτι αὐτὸν δύνασθαι φανερῶς εἰς πόλιν εἰσελθεῖν ἀλλ᾽ ἔξω ἐν ἐρήμοις τόποις ἦν καὶ ἤρχοντο πρὸς αὐτὸν πανταχόθεν
verses.king_james: But he went out, and began to publish *it* much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
